text: 6 Minute English from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 6分钟英语，来自bbclearningenglish.com

text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English. I'm Phil.
zh: 大家好，这里是BBC英语教学的6分钟英语节目，我是Phil。

text: And I'm Beth. Action figures, building bricks, stuffed cuddly animals
zh: 我是Beth。动作人偶、积木、毛绒玩偶——

text: – there's a whole world of toys that many of us have forgotten since we were children.
zh: 这是一个我们大多数人自童年以来就已淡忘的玩具世界。

text: Do you still have any toys from when you were a boy, Phil? Erm, a couple, but I gave them to my kids.
zh: Phil，你还留着小时候的玩具吗？呃，留了几件，不过都送给我孩子了。

text: Well, nowadays, grown -ups playing with toys is more common than you might think.
zh: 其实如今，成年人玩玩具比你想象的要普遍得多。

text: Recent estimates suggest that adults buying kids' toys for themselves
zh: 最新估计显示，成年人为自己购买儿童玩具

text: account for between 30 and 40 % of the entire toy market.
zh: 占整个玩具市场份额的30%到40%。

text: So many that advertisers have come up with a term for them –
zh: 人数如此之多，以至于广告商为他们创造了一个词——

text: kidults – adults who are interested in toys and games intended for children.
zh: “成人儿童”（kidults），指对本该属于孩子的玩具和游戏感兴趣的成年人。

text: Between last October and December alone, American kidults spent over $1 .5 billion on toys for themselves.
zh: 仅去年10月到12月间，美国的“成人儿童”们就为自己花费了超过15亿美元购买玩具。

text: Among the most popular were plush toys, or plushies for short, toy dolls or animals made from cloth and filled with soft material.
zh: 其中最受欢迎的是毛绒玩具，简称plushies，即用布料制成、内填柔软材料的玩偶或动物玩具。

text: And like other recent trends, the toy trend is being driven by online shoppers and social media. In this episode,
zh: 和其他近期兴起的潮流一样，这股玩具热潮也是由网购和社交媒体带动的。在本期节目中，

text: we'll meet a children's toy maker and an online influencer who are both involved in the increasing trend of adults interested in kids' toys.
zh: 我们将采访一位儿童玩具制造商和一位网络博主，他们都参与推动了成年人对儿童玩具日益增长的兴趣这一潮流。

text: We'll also learn some useful new vocabulary and
zh: 我们还将学习一些实用的新词汇，

text: remember you'll find a transcript for this episode along with all the new words and phrases on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记得在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上可以找到本期节目的文字稿以及所有新词新句。

text: But first, I have a question for you, Beth. Lego remains one of the most popular and expensive children's toys.
zh: 不过首先，Beth，我有个问题要考考你。乐高一直是最受欢迎、也是最昂贵的儿童玩具之一。

text: So, according to MSN News, what was the most expensive LEGO set ever made?
zh: 那么，根据MSN新闻的报道，史上最贵的乐高套装是什么？

text: Was it A. The LEGO Harry Potter Hogwarts School, B.
zh: 是A. 乐高哈利·波特霍格沃茨城堡，

text: The LEGO Star Wars Millennium Falcon, or C. The LEGO Eiffel Tower?
zh: B. 乐高星球大战千年隼号，还是C. 乐高埃菲尔铁塔？

text: I'm going to guess Hogwarts, because I like Harry Potter.
zh: 我猜是霍格沃茨，因为我喜欢哈利·波特。

text: OK, well, we'll find out the answer later in the programme.
zh: 好的，我们稍后在节目中揭晓答案。

text: Lisa Lee is a toy maker based in Vancouver and the owner of Leather Monsters, a brand of leather plushies which she advertises online.
zh: Lisa Lee是一位居住在温哥华的玩具制作人，也是Leather Monsters品牌的创始人，这是一个她在网上宣传的皮革毛绒玩具品牌。

text: Here, Lisa tells BBC World Service Program Business Daily how she reconnected with her love of toys as an adult.
zh: 接下来，Lisa在接受BBC世界服务《商业日报》节目采访时，讲述了她作为成年人如何重新找回对玩具的热爱。

text: Once I kind of had a stable job with disposable income, I kind of rediscovered my love for soft toys.
zh: 当我有了一份稳定的工作、有了可自由支配的收入后，我重新找回了对毛绒玩具的热爱。

text: and I started buying them again because I've always loved collecting things because you know they bring me happiness they bring me joy
zh: 于是我又开始购买它们，因为我一直喜欢收藏东西，因为它们带给我快乐、带给我幸福，

text: and I think also a big pillar of my channel and my content
zh: 而且我认为我频道内容的一大支柱

text: is reconnecting with my childhood and healing that inner child and
zh: 就是重新连接我的童年、疗愈内心的那个小孩，

text: I think the toys also play a part in that.
zh: 我觉得玩具在这方面也起到了一定作用。

text: Lisa started collecting toys as an adult with a job and disposable income.
zh: Lisa在有了工作和可自由支配收入之后，作为成年人开始收藏玩具。

text: Disposable income is the money you have left over after paying all your expenses.
zh: 可自由支配收入是指你在支付完所有开销后剩下的钱。

text: You can use it to buy whatever you like. Soft, cuddly toys make Lisa happy and help her reconnect with her inner child.
zh: 你可以用它来购买任何你喜欢的东西。柔软的毛绒玩具让Lisa感到快乐，也帮助她重新连接内心的小孩。

text: A term from psychology which describes the part of your adult personality that still feels and reacts like a child.
zh: 这是一个源自心理学的术语，指一个人成年人格中仍以孩子般的方式感受和反应的那一部分。

text: Besides the adults who sell kids toys, are the adults who buy them.
zh: 除了销售儿童玩具的成年人之外，还有购买它们的成年人。

text: Jay Glatfelter is an enthusiastic toy collector and influencer who makes YouTube videos under the name Geek Dad Life. life.
zh: Jay Glatfelter是一位热情的玩具收藏家兼网络博主，他以“Geek Dad Life”的名义制作YouTube视频。

text: Here's Jay talking about the link between COVID lockdowns and the rise in adults toy collecting to BBC World Service's Business Daily.
zh: 下面是Jay在接受BBC世界服务《商业日报》采访时，谈到新冠疫情封锁与成年人收藏玩具热潮兴起之间的联系。

text: And then what happened was a whole bunch of new collectors kind of jumped into toy collecting.
zh: 后来发生的情况是，大批新的收藏者纷纷加入了玩具收藏的行列。

text: And for those of us that were into it before, we saw the prices,
zh: 而对我们这些之前就已经在收藏的人来说，我们看到价格——

text: the secondary market prices like explode once the pandemic happened, because you had people that had time on their hands,
zh: 二级市场的价格在疫情爆发后简直是暴涨，因为人们手头有了大把时间，

text: maybe they had extra money because they're not spending it by going out and now they're putting it into collecting toys.
zh: 也许他们还有了多余的钱，因为不能出门消费了，于是就把钱投入到收藏玩具上。

text: During the pandemic, people had time on their hands, an idiom meaning to have a lot of free time and nothing to do with it.
zh: 疫情期间，人们“手头有大把时间”（time on their hands），这是一个习语，意思是有很多空闲时间却无事可做。

text: Many adults went online hoping to find the toys they remembered from childhood.
zh: 许多成年人上网寻找他们记忆中童年时的玩具。

text: As a result, a whole bunch, meaning a large number, of people started collecting toys.
zh: 结果，“a whole bunch”，意思是大量的人开始收藏玩具。

text: And slowly but surely, demand increased and prices went up.
zh: 随着时间推移，需求逐渐增加，价格也随之上涨。

text: Yes, it seems that the feeling of rediscovering your childhood, reported by many adult toy collectors,
zh: 是的，看来许多成年玩具收藏者所描述的那种重拾童年的感觉，

text: comes at a price with the cost of collectible items like Pokemon cards and jellycat plushies increasing dramatically during the pandemic.
zh: 是需要付出代价的——像宝可梦卡牌和Jellycat毛绒玩具这样的收藏品，在疫情期间价格暴涨。

text: And speaking of expensive toys, Phil, isn't it time you reveal the answer to your question?
zh: 说到昂贵的玩具，Phil，是不是该揭晓你那个问题的答案了？

text: Ah yes, I asked you what was the most expensive Lego set ever made?
zh: 啊，对，我问的是史上最贵的乐高套装是什么？

text: You said it was the Lego Hogwarts school? Well I'm afraid the answer is actually the Lego Star Wars Millennium Falcon
zh: 你说是乐高霍格沃茨城堡？恐怕答案其实是乐高星球大战千年隼号，

text: which had a UK retail price of £734 .99
zh: 根据MSN新闻报道，它在英国的零售价为734.99英镑。

text: according to MSN News. That's crazy!
zh: 太疯狂了！

text: OK, it's time to recap the vocabulary we've learned starting with kidult, an adult who likes playing with toys and games intended for children.
zh: 好了，是时候来回顾一下我们今天学到的词汇了，首先是kidult，指喜欢玩儿童玩具和游戏的成年人。

text: Plushie is short for plush toy, a toy doll or animal stuffed with a soft cloth filling.
zh: Plushie是plush toy的简称，指用柔软布料填充制成的玩偶或动物玩具。

text: Your disposable income is the amount of money you have left to buy whatever you want after paying your expenses.
zh: 你的disposable income（可自由支配收入）是指你在支付完各项开销后，剩下可以随意花用的钱。

text: Someone's inner child refers to the part of their adult personality that still feels and reacts like a child.
zh: 一个人的inner child（内心小孩）指其成年人格中仍以孩子般方式感受和反应的那部分。

text: A bunch is a number of things, often a large number of things.
zh: a bunch指一定数量的东西，通常指数量很大。

text: And finally, the idiom to have time on your hands means
zh: 最后，习语to have time on your hands的意思是

text: having free time and nothing much to do with it. it. Once again, our 6 minutes are up,
zh: 有很多空闲时间，却没什么事可做。我们的6分钟又到时间了，

text: but remember you'll find episodes about plenty more trending topics along with a quiz and worksheet for this episode on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 不过别忘了，在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上，你可以找到更多热门话题的节目，以及本期节目的测验和练习册。

text: See you there soon, but for now, it's goodbye. Bye! 6 Minute English, from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 我们很快在那里再见，不过现在，先说再见啦，拜拜！6分钟英语，来自bbclearningenglish.com
